title: JNodeProperties
lean_code_with_comments:
theorem no_arrowhead_into_input
    {G : CDMG α} {j : α} (hj : j ∈ G.J) (v : α) :
    ¬ hus G j v := by
  intro hhus
  have hjV : j ∈ G.V := by
    rcases hhus with h_hut | h_huh
    · exact (G.E_subset h_hut).2
    · exact (G.L_subset h_huh).1
  exact Set.disjoint_left.mp G.disjoint_JV hj hjV

set_option linter.unusedVariables false in
theorem input_edge_target_mem_V
    {G : CDMG α} {j : α} (hj : j ∈ G.J) {w : α}
    (h : tuh G j w) : w ∈ G.V :=
  (G.E_subset h).2

theorem input_nodes_not_adjacent
    {G : CDMG α} {j₁ j₂ : α}
    (h₁ : j₁ ∈ G.J) (h₂ : j₂ ∈ G.J) :
    ¬ Adjacent G j₁ j₂ := by
  intro hAdj
  rcases hAdj with h_tuh | h_hut | h_huh
  · exact no_arrowhead_into_input h₂ j₁ (Or.inl h_tuh)
  · exact no_arrowhead_into_input h₁ j₂ (Or.inl h_hut)
  · exact no_arrowhead_into_input h₁ j₂ (Or.inr h_huh)
lean_code_without_comments:
theorem no_arrowhead_into_input
    {G : CDMG α} {j : α} (hj : j ∈ G.J) (v : α) :
    ¬ hus G j v := by
  intro hhus
  have hjV : j ∈ G.V := by
    rcases hhus with h_hut | h_huh
    · exact (G.E_subset h_hut).2
    · exact (G.L_subset h_huh).1
  exact Set.disjoint_left.mp G.disjoint_JV hj hjV

set_option linter.unusedVariables false in
theorem input_edge_target_mem_V
    {G : CDMG α} {j : α} (hj : j ∈ G.J) {w : α}
    (h : tuh G j w) : w ∈ G.V :=
  (G.E_subset h).2

theorem input_nodes_not_adjacent
    {G : CDMG α} {j₁ j₂ : α}
    (h₁ : j₁ ∈ G.J) (h₂ : j₂ ∈ G.J) :
    ¬ Adjacent G j₁ j₂ := by
  intro hAdj
  rcases hAdj with h_tuh | h_hut | h_huh
  · exact no_arrowhead_into_input h₂ j₁ (Or.inl h_tuh)
  · exact no_arrowhead_into_input h₁ j₂ (Or.inl h_hut)
  · exact no_arrowhead_into_input h₁ j₂ (Or.inr h_huh)
lean_explanation:
The file formalises a remark from the lecture notes that bundles three structural consequences of `def_3_1`'s constraint fields into one prose paragraph. We split that paragraph into three independently citable lemmas, each phrased in the prose-level edge notation of `def_3_2` and `def_3_3` rather than in raw `G.E` / `G.L` membership.

Throughout, `α : Type*` is the ambient vertex carrier — the LN's silent universe of nodes — and `G : CDMG α` is the graph (implicit, inferred from the membership hypothesis `hj : j ∈ G.J`). The variable `j` plays the role of an input node (the LN's *j ∈ J*); `v` and `w` are auxiliary vertices.

`no_arrowhead_into_input` says that for any input node `j` and any vertex `v`, the predicate `hus G j v` cannot hold. (`hus G j v`, defined in `def_3_2`, holds iff there is an arrowhead at the first argument: either an incoming directed edge or a bidirected edge.) The proof unfolds `hus` into its two disjuncts, uses `G.E_subset` / `G.L_subset` (the `def_3_1` inclusions *E ⊆ (J ∪ V) × V* and *L ⊆ V × V*) to force `j ∈ G.V`, and applies `Set.disjoint_left.mp` to the `disjoint_JV` field to obtain a contradiction.

`input_edge_target_mem_V` says that a directed edge `tuh G j w` out of an input node must target `w ∈ G.V`; its body is the one-line projection `(G.E_subset h).2`. The declaration is preceded by `set_option linter.unusedVariables false in` because the prose-named hypothesis `hj` is not actually consumed by the proof — `E_subset` alone is strong enough — but we keep `hj` explicit to scope the lemma to input nodes.

`input_nodes_not_adjacent` concludes `¬ Adjacent G j₁ j₂` for any two input nodes, matching the LN's literal "no two nodes in *J* are adjacent". The proof case-splits on the three disjuncts of `Adjacent` (which unfolds to `tuh ∨ hut ∨ huh`) and discharges each by appealing to part (1) with the appropriate input node and disjunct injection.

Two Mathlib idioms a first-time reader may not recognise: `×ˢ` is `Set.prod`, the Cartesian product of two sets (so `E ⊆ (J ∪ V) ×ˢ V` is exactly the inclusion in `def_3_1`); and `Set.disjoint_left.mp` converts a `Disjoint A B` hypothesis into the punchier form `a ∈ A → a ∉ B`.
design_choices:
**Three separate theorems, not one conjunction.** The LN bundles three independent sub-statements into one prose paragraph for readability, but in Lean each one is wanted as a separately citable lemma. A downstream proof using "directed edges out of `J` target `V`" almost never needs "no arrowhead at `J`" on the same line. Splitting the remark into three theorems keeps each one a clean one-liner at the use site and avoids the boilerplate of destructuring a tuple of conclusions.

**Conclusions phrased in the prose-level edge notation, not raw set membership.** Each theorem's conclusion uses the `def_3_2` / `def_3_3` notation that callers will already have in their hypotheses — `hus G j v`, `tuh G j w`, `Adjacent G j₁ j₂` — rather than `(j, v) ∈ G.E` and friends. Unfolding to raw set membership is one rewrite away, but doing it in the statement would force every caller to insert a corresponding unfolding step before applying the lemma.

**Universal `v` made explicit in `no_arrowhead_into_input`.** The LN reads, paraphrased, "for any vertex *v*, the predicate `hus G j v` does not hold": the universal is outside `hus`. Making `v` explicit aligns the call-site phrasing `no_arrowhead_into_input hj v` with that reading, and lets callers with a hypothesis `h : hus G j v` pass `h` directly without `@`-syntax or relying on unification.

**Unused `hj` retained in `input_edge_target_mem_V`.** The proof does not actually use `j ∈ G.J` — the inclusion `E ⊆ (J ∪ V) ×ˢ V` alone forces `w ∈ V`. We keep `hj` as an explicit binder anyway because dropping it would broaden the statement to "any directed edge's target lies in `V`", which is a different, more general lemma. The local `set_option linter.unusedVariables false in` is just there to silence the unused-binder warning for this specific case.

**No `j₁ ≠ j₂` side-condition on `input_nodes_not_adjacent`.** The LN's "no two nodes" naturally reads as "for any two, possibly equal, input nodes". Even at `j₁ = j₂`, `Adjacent G j₁ j₁` is false: the directed disjuncts force `j₁ ∈ V` via `E_subset`, and the bidirected disjunct is ruled out by the `L_irrefl` field of `def_3_1`. Adding `j₁ ≠ j₂` would weaken the lemma without simplifying any caller.
